ul.li: Composes configs from modular YAML files

div.p: Use Case: Iterative C++ kernel/executor development BEFORE integrating with Python benchmarks

g.text: 2: Ionoc → GPUMgr: lock GPU clocks (admin elevation)
4: Ionoc → Executor: run C++ benchmark (5000 iterations)
9: Ionoc → Researcher: Performance Card (✓ NO CHANGE / ⚠ REGRESSION)

div.p: Use Case: Profile complete Python → C++ pipeline AFTER C++ integration complete

g.text: 2: IProf → Hydra: auto-load profiling config (20 iter)

ul.li: Auto-selects profiling vs full benchmark config

g.text: 5: Analysis: aggregate results, compute statistics, identify optimal configs
4: Analysis: create throughput/latency/accuracy plots, combined analysis
5: Analysis → Artifacts: save figures (PNG + SVG) to figures/
4: Report: generate HTML, embed figures (base64), create tables, executive summary
5: Report → Artifacts: save final_report.html to reports/

div.p: PRIMARY Analysis Tool: Streamlit Dashboard Real-time exploration, filtering, comparison

g.text: 3: Dashboard → Artifacts: load all CSV data
4: Artifacts → Dashboard: latency, throughput, accuracy, realtime data
6: Researcher → Dashboard: select filters (NFFT, channels, overlap)
9: Researcher → Dashboard: navigate to Ionosphere Research page
10: Dashboard: load VLF/ULF analysis (Resolution vs RTF, Temporal)
15: Researcher: iterative exploration and parameter tuning
9: Researcher: review results in MLflow UI
10b: Ionoc → Nsight: nsys profile with config
7: Hydra: compose configuration, setup multirun sweep
20: Bench: Similar process (load config, loop, log metrics, save CSV)

div.p: Similar process as latency with different parameters GPU clock locking optional
